report_name: EU Proposes 'Cybersecurity Act 2.0' to Counter Hybrid Threats and Regulate ICT Suppliers

identities.name: European Commission
identities.description: The executive body of the European Union that proposed the 'Cybersecurity Act 2.0' legislative package to strengthen the EU's defenses against cyber and hybrid threats.

identities.name: European Union Agency for Cybersecurity (ENISA)
identities.description: The EU's cybersecurity agency whose mandate and role as a central technical authority are set to be reinforced by the proposed Cybersecurity Act 2.0.

identities.name: European Parliament
identities.description: One of the EU's legislative bodies involved in the negotiation and amendment process for the proposed Cybersecurity Act 2.0.

identities.name: EU Council
identities.description: One of the EU's legislative bodies involved in the negotiation and amendment process for the proposed Cybersecurity Act 2.0.

identities.name: Essential and Important Entities (under NIS2)
identities.description: A class of approximately 28,700 companies in critical sectors that will be affected by amendments to the NIS2 Directive, which aim to clarify risk-management obligations and streamline reporting.

identities.name: High-Risk ICT Suppliers
identities.description: A class of Information and Communications Technology (ICT) suppliers, particularly those from designated third countries, that could face market access restrictions under the proposed Cybersecurity Act 2.0 to mitigate supply chain risks and foreign interference.
